task:
Configure CORS on your Kubo node:

ipfs config --json API.HTTPHeaders.Access-Control-Allow-Origin '["https://app.cipherbox.cc", "http://localhost:5173"]'
ipfs config --json API.HTTPHeaders.Access-Control-Allow-Methods '["POST"]'
ipfs config --json API.HTTPHeaders.Access-Control-Allow-Headers '["Authorization", "Content-Type"]'

Then restart your Kubo node.

<result>I now have a comprehensive picture of the entire E2E authentication system. Here is the complete report.

---

## E2E Authentication in CipherBox: Complete Overview

### Directory Structure

```
tests/web-e2e/
├── fixtures/files/        - Static test fixtures (test-image.png)
├── page-objects/
│   ├── base.page.ts       - BasePage with goto(), waitForPageLoad(), getByTestId()
│   ├── login.page.ts      - LoginPage (email/OTP + wallet flows)
│   ├── file-browser/      - File browser POs
│   ├── dialogs/           - Dialog POs
│   └── pages/             - bin.page.ts, invite.page.ts
├── tests/
│   ├── full-workflow.spec.ts
│   ├── mfa-flows.spec.ts
│   ├── recycle-bin.spec.ts
│   ├── search-workflow.spec.ts
│   ├── wallet-login.spec.ts
│   ├── conflict-detection.spec.ts
│   ├── invite-link-workflow.spec.ts
│   └── sharing-workflow.spec.ts
└── utils/
    ├── wallet-login-helpers.ts   - Core auth helper
    ├── multi-account-wallet.ts   - Multi-account setup
    ├── mfa-helpers.ts            - MFA/device approval helpers
    ├── api-helpers.ts            - Stub (not yet implemented)
    ├── test-files.ts             - File fixture utilities
    └── conflict-helpers.ts
```

---

### How Auth Works: Two Distinct Mechanisms

#### 1. Wallet Login (Primary — used by almost all specs)

All functional specs (`full-workflow`, `mfa-flows`, `recycle-bin`, `search-workflow`, `sharing-workflow`, etc.) use the **mock wallet** path:

**Key files:**
- `/Users/michael/Code/cipher-box-phase-20/tests/web-e2e/utils/wallet-login-helpers.ts`
- `/Users/michael/Code/cipher-box-phase-20/tests/web-e2e/utils/multi-account-wal

### No Global Setup / No Stored Auth State

The `playwright.config.ts` has no `globalSetup` and no `storageState` — each test suite logs in fresh via the UI in its own `beforeAll`. Tests run serially (`fullyParallel: false, workers: 1`) with `retries: 0`.</result>

### Key Selectors for Auth

| UI Element | Selector |
|---|---|
| Email input | `[data-testid="email-input"]` |
| Send OTP button | `[data-testid="send-otp-button"]` |
| OTP input | `[data-testid="otp-input"]` |
| Verify button | `[data-testid="verify-button"]` |
| Wallet login button | `[data-testid="wallet-login-button"]` |
| Connector list | `.wallet-connector-list` |
| Individual connector | `.wallet-connector-option` (has text `"Mock Wallet"`) |
| Cancel connector | `.wallet-connector-cancel` |
| Device waiting screen | `[data-testid="device-waiting"]` |
| Post-login confirmation | `.file-list[role="grid"]` or `[data-testid="empty-state"]` |

### How Auth Works: Two Distinct Mechanisms

#### 1. Wallet Login (Primary — used by almost all specs)

All functional specs (`full-workflow`, `mfa-flows`, `recycle-bin`, `search-workflow`, `sharing-workflow`, etc.) use the **mock wallet** path:

**Key files:**
- `/Users/michael/Code/cipher-box-phase-20/tests/web-e2e/utils/wallet-login-helpers.ts`
- `/Users/michael/Code/cipher-box-phase-20/tests/web-e2e/utils/multi-account-wallet.ts`

The pattern in every `beforeAll`:
```ts
account = createTestAccount();           // generates random secp256k1 key
await setupMockWallet(page, account);    // installs EIP-6963 mock wallet via @johanneskares/wallet-mock
const result = await loginViaWallet(page, { timeout: 60_000 });
```

`loginViaWallet()` drives the real UI:
1. Navigates to `/`
2. Waits for `[data-testid="wallet-login-button"]` to become enabled (Core Kit init)
3. Clicks the wallet button to reveal `.wallet-connector-list`
4. Clicks `.wallet-connector-option` with text `"Mock Wallet"`
5. `Promise.race`s between `waitForURL('**/files')` and `[data-testid="device-waiting"]`
6. On success, waits for `.file-list[role="grid"]` or `[data-testid="empty-state"]` to confirm vault initialization

Each test run generates a **random private key** → unique Ethereum address → unique backend `userId` → fresh DKG identity on Web3Auth Sapphire Devnet. This avoids any state leakage between runs.

The mock wallet uses a **local-only viem transport** (no network calls) that handles `eth_chainId`, `net_version`, `eth_blockNumber`, `eth_getBalance` — preventing CI hangs from real RPC timeouts.

`wallet-login.spec.ts` also tests with the deterministic **Hardhat account #0** key (`0xac0974bec...`) for UI-level wallet tests (TC09–TC12) that don't need a fresh identity.

#### 2. Email + OTP Login (Supported in UI, not used directly by current specs)

`/Users/michael/Code/cipher-box-phase-20/tests/web-e2e/page-objects/login.page.ts` exposes:
```ts
loginPage.enterEmail(email)    // fills [data-t
